workItem.title: Preserve dirty workspaces and durably recover interrupted AgentRuns
workItem.body:

## Context

A controlled interruption of Symphony Issues #10 and #11 removed their worktrees while they still contained uncommitted implementation changes. The audit ledger retained execution history but no durable workspace checkpoint, so the run could not resume from the exact filesystem state.

## Expected outcome

Symphony never deletes a workspace containing uncommitted changes, records the preservation decision, and resumes the same AgentRun from the preserved or durably reconstructed state after interruption.

## Scope

- Fail closed when local or remote Git workspaces are dirty
- Preserve the workspace for retry instead of deleting it
- Add regression tests for clean, dirty and unreadable Git state
- Document the recovery invariant and operational response
- Add a durable checkpoint path so loss of the X1 does not lose uncommitted progress

## Out of scope

- Bypass terminal cleanup for clean workspaces
- Store secrets or ignored runtime caches in checkpoints

## Acceptance criteria

- Dirty workspaces survive cleanup and can be reused by the next attempt
- Clean terminal workspaces are still removed
- Git status failures block deletion rather than assuming the workspace is clean
- The interrupted run can be reconstructed without relying on Codex thread memory
- Regression tests and recovery documentation are committed
